extraction.one_line_summary: ZAKARIN attacked from the breakaway with 5km to go on the final climb, winning solo while ROGLIČ rode away from NIBALI's group in the GC battle
tactical_patterns: LANDA accelerated 15km from finish to test GC group, forcing immediate splits before riders regrouped on easier gradients
ZAKARIN launched winning move with 5km remaining knowing steepest terrain still ahead, exploiting diminishing breakaway cooperation timing
ROGLIČ attacked NIBALI's group on steep sections but was marked immediately, revealing both are relatively matched on sustained climbing
LÓPEZ mechanical at critical moment required full effort to rejoin GC group, potentially burning reserves needed for final kilometers

rider_signals.ZAKARIN Ilnur: positive: Won from breakaway with superior positioning judgment on steep gradients; Katusha finally secured first 2019 World Tour victory
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: positive: Early acceleration 15km out immediately gapped GC riders including FORMOLO, showing ability to create splits on demand
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: Attacked NIBALI's group and finished 2:57 down with NIBALI, confirming competitive GC position despite testing pace
rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: neutral: Marked ROGLIČ attacks without difficulty, finished same time but didn't attempt to distance Slovenian when tested
rider_signals.YATES Simon: negative: Lost five minutes to stage winner, suffered badly when pace lifted early, appears out of GC contention
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: positive: Finished third behind LANDA in breakaway selection, demonstrating climbing strength within leading group composition
rider_signals.LÓPEZ Miguel Ángel: negative: Mechanical on climb forced desperate chase back to GC group, expending significant energy before critical finale
rider_signals.POLANC Jan: neutral: Pink jersey holder finished 3:17 down but ahead of rivals, maintaining sufficient margin in leader's jersey
